LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS EVA CHAO AND AMY CHIANG'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED WITH 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT THAT THE PARTIES DID NOT INTEND BY THE RECITAL IN THEIR SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT TO SETTLE SOME OR ALL OF THE STATED CLAIMS CREATES AN ISSUE OF CONTRACT INTERPRETATION THAT CANNOT BE RESOLVED ON DEMURRER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR # 8189 301/PJB

ATTY. JOHN R. WILEY, TEL. NO. 863-0500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS AND MOVING PARTIES.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS EVA CHAO AND AMY CHIANG'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED WITH 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. Notice Of Motion Andmotion For Judgment On The Pleadings;
Off Calendar Jul-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
John Wiley, Esq., 415-863-0800 for defendants and moving parties Eva Chao and Amy Chiang
Ronald Losch, Esq. and Mark Meyer, Esq. of Losch & Ehrlich, 415-956-8400 for plaintiff and opposing party

RULING:
ARGUED and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows --
DEFENDANT EVA CHAO, AMY CHIANG'S Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is GRANTED. HAVING HAD JUDGMENT ENTERED ON AN AGREEMENT SETTLING ALL DISPUTES WITH THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT, PLAINTIFF IS BARRED BY RES JUDICATA FROM SUING THE ASSIGNOR ON THE BASIS OF THE ASSIGNEE'S BREACHES FOR WHICH THE ASSIGNOR IS ONLY LIABLE IF THE ASSIGNEE FAILED TO PERFORM. PLAINTIFF HAS 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT. Moving party to submit form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Candace Yount, CSR # 2737 (301/PJB)
